summary.scope: ATM framework runAtm/runAtmMap callsites at stable boundaries only
summary.validatorResult: PASS — semantic agent pass, bad agents blocked, sweep rewrite verified
summary.principle: Only wrap stable boundaries (CLI entrypoints, guard/validate gates, registry generator) with readable refs. Internal helpers stay direct to preserve readability.

wrapWithRunAtm: CLI command runner boundaries (atm.ts dispatch + per-command runners)
guard/validate/test public gate entrypoints (atm.mjs guard/validate/test entry)
registry generator and release build orchestrators (atm-chart, build-release)
doNotWrap: private utility modules (e.g. shared.ts message/makeResult helpers)
tightly coupled internal calls within the same atom
boundaryClassification.rationale: runAtm is for stable, capability-shaped entrypoints. Wrapping internals creates ID-shaped names (e.g. atmCore0005Atom) that hurt readability without semantic gain.

migratedBoundaries.atomRef: guards reference atom-validator-* by semantic name
validators reference atom-validator-* by semantic name

validatorEvidence.result: ok (semantic agent pass, bad agents blocked, sweep rewrite verified)
checks: semanticAgentPass: PASS — all runAtm first-arg refs are semantic, not ID-shaped
badAgentsBlocked: PASS — ID-shaped refs (like atmCore0005Atom) are rejected by the readability gate
sweepRewriteVerified: PASS — the sweep behavior's rewrite of bad refs produces semantic output

callsiteInventoryProcess.approach: atom-ref-readability validator inspects all packages/, scripts/, integrations/ source files
patterns: runAtm(<ref>, ...) — ref must be semantic word, not ID-shaped
atom-callsite-inventory.json tracks every callsite and its semantic status

rollbackPlan.approach: Each migration is reversible by reverting the specific commit. No structural change to runtime contracts.
